description: Detailed system overview for API, pipeline, ClickHouse, and tracing health across the GitHub analyzer stack.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data
SELECT uniq(repo_name) AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data
SELECT dateDiff('second', max(ingested_at), now()) AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data
SELECT count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data WHERE ingested_at >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
SELECT toStartOfHour(ingested_at) AS time, count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data WHERE ingested_at >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR GROUP BY time ORDER BY time
SELECT event_type AS metric, count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data WHERE ingested_at >= now() - INTERVAL 7 DAY GROUP BY event_type ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 8

options.content:
### Trace Drilldown
- Open **Grafana Explore** from the dashboard link, or use the API response header `X-Trace-Explore-Url` for a prefilled trace search.
- Every API response exposes `X-Trace-Id` for correlation.
- FastAPI requests and outbound `httpx` calls are exported to Tempo over OTLP.
- Use the traces to inspect slow AI calls to Ollama or GitHub API fetches.

title: System Overview
